reviews.review_msg: Very passionate and very interesting class. Many take it for an easy grade and don't show up, if you are coming in with ignorant mindset that, "it's easy." May I remind you, like every other class you gotta show up, you gotta study and you actually have to try! I loved this course, if you do the work you get the grade you deserve.

Notable professor during undergrad. 
The comments complaining about being guests in an Indigenous-led urban garden and experiencing practical fieldwork missed the entire point of the class. He's genuinely passionate, so it's not simply a “fun” class for an easy ‘A'. You must practice what you learn, including building your relationship with Nature.

Bob was an ok prof, lectures were very dry but interesting. Taken during intersession, and honestly glad it wasn't any longer. One field trip was literally just us providing free labour to his friend to help her garden, which was weird lol. Has criminally vague assignment details and is a v hard marker with super specific criteria he won't provide

Bob is nice, very dad energy but he also reminds me of my dad in the way that I think he forgets what he says ten seconds after he says it. Bob stated, "you only need to know the plant genus for the quiz" and then proceeded to create a hunger games level quiz that required me to know deep extensive plant knowledge scored out of 46. not too pleased.
Bob is such a genuine guy, and INDG322 was by far the best class I've taken at SFU
took this as an intersession course so it was kinda condensed but Bob made it a great 6 weeks! amazing prof!

Bob has been my favourite prof at SFU so far. He is kind and he deserves to be recognized for it. He makes the classroom a fun and safe place to learn. INDG 332 requires little to no background knowledge on ethnobotany. Follow his criteria to a key and you will be just fine.
Bob is one of the best professors at SFU

Bob is a very sweet guy and makes the material very straight forward. His assignments are very flexible and don't have too many requirements which can be good or bad depending your preferences. I had a couple grievances like making us go to North Van for a "field trip" to dig up his friend's garden which was weird, but otherwise I would take again
